name: proxysql
explanation: ProxySQL exposes its admin interface via the MySQL wire protocol on a separate admin port. The check connects with pymysql and queries stats_* tables in the admin schema, so a username and password (the ProxySQL admin user) are required. Both are marked required: true with no defaults in the spec, and there is no unauthenticated mode. Default port is also user-supplied (no default) — typically 6032, but not enforced by the check.
notes: Pattern D (spec-required credentials). MySQL wire protocol means a TCP banner probe could detect a MySQL-shaped server, but auth is still mandatory before any stats can be read.
